name: Higa de Monreal Loop
name_original: Salinas de Ibargoiti - Ekisoain – circular bordeando el Higa de Monreal
description: This 14.2-mile mountain biking route circles Higa de Monreal, offering challenging climbs, technical terrain, and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ibargoiti, located in Navarra, Spain, offers routes through a natural setting characterized by mountainous terrain and river valleys. The area lies between the Sierras de Izaga and Izco, featuring landscapes with arboreal vegetation including pine, oak, and beech forests. This region provides varied terrain suitable for mountain biking, from forested paths to open valley routes. Ibargoiti is situated within a broader area known for extensive trail networks and diverse ecosystems. Best mountain bike trails around Ibargoiti The most popular mountain bike trail is Higa de Monreal Loop, a 14.2 miles (22.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding peaks. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of the Elorz Valley – El Oasis de Carmelo loop from Salinas de Ibargoiti / Getze Ibargoiti, a moderate 31.7 miles (50.9 km) path. This route explores the scenic Elorz Valley. Local mountain bikers also love the Otano Gate – Castillo de Tiebas loop from Salinas de Ibargoiti / Getze Ibargoiti, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards historical landmarks, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Ibargoiti is defined by mountainous forests, river valleys, and diverse ecosystems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Ibargoiti are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ibargoiti's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ibargoiti?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Ibargoiti, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The region's diverse landscapes, from mountainous forests to river valleys, provide a rich environment for cycling.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Ibargoiti?
A: The trails around Ibargoiti cater to a range of abilities. You'll find 1 easy route, 19 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989975255/higa-de-monreal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higa de Monreal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38196624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otano Gate – Castillo de Tiebas loop from Salinas de Ibargoiti / Getze Ibargoiti&lt;/a&gt; offers a more difficult challenge.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Ibargoiti area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Ibargoiti are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31878290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Elorz Valley – El Oasis de Carmelo loop from Salinas de Ibargoiti / Getze Ibargoiti&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10517558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Salinas de Ibargoiti / Getze Ibargoiti&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Ibargoiti?
A: Ibargoiti is nestled between the Sierras de Izaga and Izco, offering mountainous terrain with arboreal vegetation like pine, oak, and beech forests. You'll also encounter scenic river valleys, such as the Elorz Valley. The broader Navarra region provides diverse ecosystems, from forests and gorges to cultivated fields.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38196624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otano Gate – Castillo de Tiebas loop from Salinas de Ibargoiti / Getze Ibargoiti&lt;/a&gt; leads towards historical landmarks like Castillo de Tiebas. The historic Camino de Santiago also passes through the Ibargoiti valley, including towns like Izco and Salinas, offering a blend of sport and cultural exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Ibargoiti?
A: Thanks to Navarra's climate, influenced by both Mediterranean and oceanic conditions, mountain biking in Ibargoiti is possible year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for extended rides.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Ibargoiti?
A: The trails in Ibargoiti are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural setting, and the opportunities to explore both forested paths and open valley routes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the area around Ibargoiti features impressive natural attractions. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2933330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Lumbier (Lumbier Gorge)&lt;/a&gt;, known for its dramatic cliffs and the chance to spot vultures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Diablozulo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural highlight nearby.

Q: Can I find easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Ibargoiti?
A: While most routes are moderate, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available, making it suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These trails typically feature less elevation gain and smoother surfaces.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in Ibargoiti?
A: The natural resources of the region support diverse wildlife. You might spot wild boar, roe deer, and various birds of prey, including vultures, especially in areas like the Foz de Lumbier Nature Reserve.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 4 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38196624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otano Gate – Castillo de Tiebas loop from Salinas de Ibargoiti / Getze Ibargoiti&lt;/a&gt;, for example, covers 25.5 miles (41.0 km) with significant elevation changes, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Is Ibargoiti well-connected by public transport for mountain bikers?
A: Ibargoiti is located approximately 22 kilometers from Pamplona, which serves as a regional hub. While direct public transport options to Ibargoiti itself might be limited, accessing the area via Pamplona and then using local transport or a short drive is a common approach for visitors.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Salinas de Ibargoiti - Ekisoain – circular bordeando el Higa de Monreal trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite demanding, especially on the ascent of Higa de Monreal. Riders can expect a broken and uneven road with potholes, which provides a technical challenge ideal for mountain biking. The route likely combines various surfaces, from rough mountain roads to potentially smoother sections as it circles the mountain.

faqs.question: How challenging is the climb up Higa de Monreal on this route?
faqs.answer: The climb up Higa de Monreal is renowned for being challenging, with constant steep gradients. Sections can reach a 13-14% incline, and the road is noted for its rough condition, making it a demanding physical and technical test for mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several points of interest. These include the historic Medieval bridge of Salinas de Ibargoiti, the Alto de Ekisoain, and the Alto de Karbatoki Wind Farm. You'll also ride along scenic sections like the Peña de la Guardia Forest Path and the Southern Descent Trail of Higa de Monreal.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, from the summit of Higa de Monreal, riders are rewarded with unbeatable panoramic views that can extend across the Pamplona basin and even to the Pyrenees. The circular nature of the route also offers diverse scenic elements throughout.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact route are not provided, but it typically starts and ends in Salinas de Ibargoiti or Ekisoain. Look for public parking areas within these villages to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike route in Navarre.

faqs.question: Is the Salinas de Ibargoiti - Ekisoain – circular bordeando el Higa de Monreal trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in Navarre, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Given the technical nature and potential for other users, ensure your dog is well-behaved and can handle the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The region of Navarre offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder weather or snow at higher elevations, making spring and autumn ideal.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will ride sections of the Aragonese Way, Xabierraldia (Iruña - Xabierko gaztelua), Peña de la Guardia, and Camino de Ilarkoa.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route typically takes around 2 hours and 27 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area without requiring an out-and-back return on the same path.
